# PitlaneStats

> The most comprehensive Formula 1 statistics database on the web.

PitlaneStats covers the complete history of Formula 1 from 1950 to 2026, including every driver, constructor, race, circuit, and season.

## Key Facts

- 818 drivers profiled with career statistics
- 1,158 races documented with full results
- 205 constructors/teams tracked
- 78 circuits worldwide
- 77 seasons from 1950 to 2026
- Data sourced from the Jolpica F1 API (Ergast compatible)

## Sections

- [Seasons](https://pitlanestats.com/seasons/index.html): Championship seasons from 1950 to 2026, including race calendars, standings, and results
- [Drivers](https://pitlanestats.com/drivers/index.html): Career profiles for every F1 driver, including wins, podiums, poles, and championship results
- [Teams](https://pitlanestats.com/constructors/index.html): Constructor profiles with championship history and race results
- [Circuits](https://pitlanestats.com/circuits/index.html): Track information for every Grand Prix venue
- [Wet Weather Stats](https://pitlanestats.com/wet-weather/index.html): F1 rain race statistics — most wet-weather wins, best wet win percentage, wet-weather specialists, and the most dramatic rain races in F1 history
- [Points System History](https://pitlanestats.com/points-history/index.html): The complete history of the F1 points system from 1950 to today — every scoring change, fastest-lap and sprint points, dropped scores, and the championships that would have had different winners under different systems
- [Sitemap](https://pitlanestats.com/sitemap.xml): Full XML sitemap of all 2,951 pages

## Notes

- This is a static site hosted on GitHub Pages
- Not affiliated with Formula 1, FIA, or any team
- Data is factual and statistical in nature
